senses.definition: Located behind the clavicle (collarbone).
examples: The anesthetist chose a retroclavicular approach for the nerve block.
A retroclavicular incision was made to access the brachial plexus.

senses.definition: A backward displacement or closing of a body part, especially a tooth or jaw, relative to its usual position.
examples: The dentist noted a slight retroclusion of the patient's lower jaw.
Corrective appliances were used to treat the retroclusion.

senses.definition: The supposed ability to gain knowledge of past events without using normal senses.
examples: The medium claimed to experience retrocognition during the séance.
Stories of retrocognition are common in parapsychology.

senses.definition: Plural of retrocognition: instances of supposed knowledge of past events gained without normal senses.
examples: The book collects several reported retrocognitions from different cultures.
Researchers tried to test the accuracy of these retrocognitions.

senses.definition: Relating to or involving retrocognition.
examples: She described vivid retrocognitive visions of the old battlefield.
The study focused on retrocognitive experiences during dreams.

senses.definition: Situated behind the colon.
examples: The surgeon identified a retrocolic abscess.
Retrocolic hernias can be difficult to diagnose.

senses.definition: A supposed state of consciousness in which a person becomes aware of past events beyond normal memory.
examples: The author links retroconsciousness to mystical experiences of time.
Some theories of retroconsciousness overlap with ideas about past-life memories.

senses.definition: Serving to connect or link something to an earlier part, especially in logic or grammar.
examples: The retrocopulant element in the sentence refers back to the first clause.
He analyzed the retrocopulant function of the auxiliary verb.

senses.definition: The act or process of linking something back to an earlier part, especially in logical or grammatical structures.
examples: The argument depends on careful retrocopulation of later claims with earlier premises.
Retrocopulation in the text helps maintain coherence across chapters.

senses.definition: Situated behind a rib or the ribs.
examples: The scan revealed a retrocostal mass.
The surgeon explored the retrocostal space for bleeding.

senses.definition: To connect a device or circuit back into a previous stage of a system.
examples: The engineer decided to retrocouple the sensor output into the control loop.
They retrocoupled the amplifier to stabilize the signal.

senses.definition: A device used to feed a signal or effect back into an earlier part of a system.
examples: The circuit includes a retrocoupler to improve stability.
A faulty retrocoupler caused oscillations in the control system.

senses.definition: Curving backward or in a reverse direction.
examples: The bird's retrocurved claws help it grip tree bark.
The tool has a retrocurved tip for reaching behind structures.

senses.definition: Simple past tense and past participle of retread: to have walked again on the same path, especially through something like snow or mud.
examples: We retrod our steps back to the cabin before dark.
They carefully retrod the path they had taken that morning.

senses.definition: To give something a date earlier than the actual or current date.
examples: The contract was retrodated to the start of the month.
They agreed to retrodate the insurance policy.

senses.definition: Past participle of retread: walked again on the same path.
examples: Their retrodden footsteps were visible in the snow.
The retrodden path through the field showed how often people passed that way.

senses.definition: A backward displacement or bending of an organ from its usual position.
examples: The scan showed retrodeviation of the uterus.
Retrodeviation can cause discomfort in some patients.

senses.definition: To use present information or ideas to infer or explain something that happened in the past.
examples: From the current data, scientists can retrodict past climate patterns.
Investigators retrodicted the sequence of events leading to the accident.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of retrodict: inferred or explained something about the past from present information.
examples: The model successfully retrodicted several known historical temperatures.
They retrodicted the star's earlier position using its current motion.

senses.definition: Present participle of retrodict: using current information to infer or explain past events.
examples: By retrodicting from present conditions, they estimated ancient sea levels.
The theory is useful for retrodicting earlier stages of the universe.

senses.definition: The act of explaining or inferring a past event from information available now.
examples: Retrodiction is an important tool in cosmology.
The theory gains support through successful retrodiction of known events.

senses.definition: Plural of retrodiction: explanations or inferences about past events based on present information.
examples: The model's retrodictions match historical records closely.
Competing theories were judged by the accuracy of their retrodictions.

senses.definition: Relating to retrodiction, especially the ability of a theory or model to explain known past events.
examples: The theory has strong retrodictive power.
Retrodictive success is one sign that the model is useful.

senses.definition: Third person singular of retrodict: uses present information to infer or explain a past event.
examples: The model retrodicts the observed distribution of galaxies.
This approach retrodicts past climate changes accurately.

senses.definition: Automatically sending a signal back in the direction it came from without needing to know that direction.
examples: The radar uses a retrodirective array to return the signal.
Retrodirective antennas can simplify tracking systems.

senses.definition: A backward movement or shifting of an organ or body part from its normal position.
examples: Retrodisplacement of the uterus may cause pain.
The X-ray showed retrodisplacement of the vertebra.

senses.definition: A form of reasoning that moves from observed facts to a possible explanation or hypothesis about their cause.
examples: The philosopher described retroduction as reasoning to the best explanation.
Scientific discovery often relies on retroduction from data to theory.

senses.definition: Located behind the dura mater, the outer membrane covering the brain and spinal cord.
examples: The surgeon noted a retrodural collection of blood.
Retrodural lesions can compress the spinal cord.

senses.definition: Situated behind the esophagus.
examples: The scan showed a retroesophageal course of the subclavian artery.
A retroesophageal mass was compressing the esophagus.

senses.definition: To fire a rocket engine in the opposite direction to slow down or change the path of a spacecraft or missile.
examples: The spacecraft will retrofire its main engine before re-entry.
Controllers prepared to retrofire the booster stage.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of retrofire: fired a rocket engine in the opposite direction to slow or redirect a vehicle.
examples: The capsule retrofired successfully and began to descend.
They retrofired the thrusters to enter orbit.

senses.definition: Third person singular of retrofire: fires a rocket engine in the opposite direction to slow or redirect a vehicle.
examples: The spacecraft retrofires its engines before landing.
If the system fails, the module automatically retrofires.

senses.definition: Present participle of retrofire: firing a rocket engine in the opposite direction to slow or redirect a vehicle.
examples: By retrofiring at the right moment, they reduced the spacecraft's speed.
Retrofiring the boosters allowed a controlled descent.

senses.definition: To add new parts or equipment to something old so it works better or meets new standards.
examples: They retrofitted the old building with modern insulation.
The company plans to retrofit its trucks with cleaner engines.

senses.definition: An update or improvement that is added to an older product or system.
examples: The safety retrofit will cost several million dollars.
They offered a free retrofit for older models.

senses.definition: Third person singular of retrofit: adds new parts or equipment to something old so it works better or meets new standards.
examples: The city retrofits its older bridges to improve safety.
The firm retrofits buildings to reduce energy use.

senses.definition: Plural of retrofit: updates or improvements added to older products or systems.
examples: Energy-efficient retrofits can cut costs.
The government supports retrofits for older homes.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of retrofit: added new parts or equipment to something old so it worked better or met new standards.
examples: The plant was retrofitted to reduce emissions.
Many schools have been retrofitted with air-conditioning.

senses.definition: Present participle of retrofit: adding new parts or equipment to something old so it works better or meets new standards.
examples: They are retrofitting old buildings to make them more energy-efficient.
Retrofitting the factory will take several months.

senses.definition: The process of adding new parts or equipment to something old to improve it or meet new standards.
examples: Seismic retrofitting is essential in earthquake-prone areas.
The program supports retrofitting of old apartment blocks.

senses.definition: Plural of retrofitting: projects or processes where new parts or equipment are added to old structures or systems.
examples: The city has planned several retrofittings for public housing.
Energy-efficient retrofittings are encouraged by the government.

senses.definition: Bent or turned backward.
examples: The tip of the leaf is retroflected.
In this species, the spines are strongly retroflected.

senses.definition: A bending or turning backward of an organ or structure.
examples: Retroflection of the uterus is common and often harmless.
The plant shows retroflection of its leaf tips.

senses.definition: Plural of retroflection: backward bendings or turnings of organs or structures.
examples: Various retroflections of the vertebral column were observed.
The study described several uterine retroflections.

senses.definition: Bent or curved backward.
examples: The bird's retroflex claws help it grip branches.
The instrument has a retroflex tip to reach behind folds.

senses.definition: Describing a speech sound made with the tongue curled back toward the roof of the mouth.
examples: In Hindi, several consonants are retroflex.
Learners often find retroflex sounds difficult to pronounce.

senses.definition: A speech sound produced with the tongue curled back toward the roof of the mouth.
examples: The language contrasts several retroflexes with dental sounds.
She struggled to distinguish the retroflex from the alveolar sound.

senses.definition: Bent or curved backward.
examples: The uterus is retroflexed in many women.
The instrument has a retroflexed tip for better access.

senses.definition: Third person singular of retroflex: bends or curves something backward.
examples: The device retroflexes the catheter tip inside the vessel.
The animal retroflexes its tail when threatened.

senses.definition: Plural of retroflex: speech sounds made with the tongue curled back toward the roof of the mouth.
examples: The language has four retroflexes in its consonant system.
Learners often confuse the retroflexes with dental consonants.

senses.definition: Present participle of retroflex: bending or curving something backward.
examples: Retroflexing the catheter allows better visualization.
The animal responded by retroflexing its spine.

senses.definition: The act or state of bending or curving backward.
examples: Retroflexion of the uterus may be seen on ultrasound.
The endoscope allows retroflexion to view behind folds.

senses.definition: Plural of retroflexion: backward bendings or curvings of organs or structures.
examples: Different types of uterine retroflexions are described in the text.
Spinal retroflexions can affect posture.

senses.definition: A backward flow of a fluid, particles, or energy.
examples: The model predicts a retroflux of particles in this region.
Retroflux in the system can lead to contamination.

senses.definition: Having a shape that is turned or directed backward.
examples: The fossil shows a retroform curve in the spine.
Retroform features are common in this group of shells.

senses.definition: To bend or break back, especially referring to a structure or ray.
examples: The wave is retrofracted at the boundary.
The force retrofracted the branch near its base.
